sanity.title: Sanity System
sanity.text:
Every player begins with 30/30 Sanity. Certain areas, creatures, and abilities can reduce your Sanity. When your Sanity drops, you risk gaining Tiers of Insanity.

Certain locations accelerate mental decay such as Scyphozia.
• Every 30 real-life minutes spent in Scyphozia → lose 1d8 Sanity
• Every 30 minutes spent in sunlight → gain 1d6 Sanity

Tiers of Insanity:
You gain 1 Tier for every 1/3 of your maximum Sanity lost.

Formula:
Max Sanity ÷ 3 = 1 Tier threshold

Examples:
30 Max Sanity → 10 Sanity lost = 1 Tier
60 Max Sanity → 20 Sanity lost = 1 Tier

Tier 1 – False Reality:
All skill rolls are made with disadvantage.

Tier 2 – Tremors:
At the start of your turn, roll 1d20.
• 10 or lower → lose your bonus action
• 11 or higher → lose your main action

Tier 3 – Complete Collapse:
Every 15 real-life minutes, you lose 25% of your maximum HP. This cannot be regained while insane.
If you remain at Tier 3 for 1 full hour, you die.

traits.title: Traits System
traits.text:
Traits are the subtle marks of growth that awaken within as one's Power deepens.

Traits appear at Power 2, 4, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12, 14, 16, 18, 19, and 20.

Each trait point may be placed into one of four paths:

• Vitality – Increases your max HP by 1 hit die per point, up to 6 total
• Erudition – Increases your Ether by 20 and Ether Regen by 10 per point, up to +120 Ether and +60 Regen
• Proficiency – Every 2 points gives +1 weapon damage and +1 hit bonus
• Songchant – Every 2 points gives +1 mantra damage, +1 hit bonus, and +1 spell save DC

weapons.title: Weapon System
weapons.text:
Weapons have properties based on their category: Light, Medium, Heavy, or Hybrid.

Weapons use crit points based on star rating from 0 to 3.

• Natural 20 → automatically perform a critical and regain 1 crit point
• Natural 1 → lose 1 crit point

Even weapons that are otherwise identical may have different critical effects, making each weapon unique.
